name: Dolmades (Stuffed Grape Leaves)
recipeIngredient: 20 large grape leaves from a jar, available in some supermarkets and most specialty food shops
salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse each of the grape leaves well, snip off the stems, and pat dry.
2. Set aside, stacked on a plate and covered with plastic wrap.
3. In a skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over moderate heat.
4. Cook the onion until soft. Add garlic and cook for another minute.
5. Add cinnamon stick and rice, stirring to coat and combine.
6. Stir in currants, pine nuts, mint, salt, pepper, and 1/2 cup chicken stock.
7. Bring mixture to a simmer and cook until all liquid is evaporated, stirring occasionally.
8. Remove skillet from heat, discard cinnamon stick, and allow to cool completely.
9. Place grape leaf smooth side down on work surface with stem end toward you.
10. Place tablespoon of cooled rice filling near the stem end and fold in sides over the filling.
11. Roll up the leaf, not too tightly as rice will continue to absorb liquid and expand during next cooking step.
12. Repeat for remaining grape leaves.
13. Transfer dolmades to a skillet large enough to contain them in a single layer.
14. In a bowl combine remaining chicken stock and olive oil with lemon juice.
15. Pour liquid over dolmades, adding additional water if necessary to just barely cover.
16. Bring liquid to simmer and cook, covered, until tender when pierced with fork (about 30 minutes).
17. Remove skillet from heat, uncover, and allow to cool.
18. Serve warm, at room temperature, or chilled.
